questions.q: In Hindu tradition, Mohini is the enchanting female form taken by which deity?
questions.explanation: Mohini is the bewitching female avatar assumed by Lord Vishnu.

questions.q: Vishnu took the form of Mohini to recover which precious substance after the churning of the ocean?
questions.explanation: Vishnu became Mohini specifically to reclaim the amrita, the nectar of immortality.

questions.q: The Mohini episode arises directly from which great cosmic event?
options: The burning of the Khandava forest
The destruction of the three cities
The churning of the ocean of milk
questions.explanation: Mohini appears during the Samudra Manthana, the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: After the amrita appeared, who seized the pot, prompting Vishnu to take the Mohini form?
questions.explanation: The asuras snatched the pot of nectar, so Vishnu became Mohini to recover it.

questions.q: As Mohini, to whom did Vishnu serve the nectar of immortality?
questions.explanation: Mohini beguiled the demons and served the amrita only to the devas.

questions.q: The name 'Mohini' is derived from a Sanskrit root meaning what?
questions.explanation: The name Mohini comes from 'moha', meaning enchantment, delusion, or bewilderment.

questions.q: Which Purana most famously narrates the story of Mohini distributing the nectar?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana is the principal text recounting the Mohini episode.

questions.q: Which demon disguised himself among the gods to secretly drink the nectar served by Mohini?
questions.explanation: The demon Rahu, also called Svarbhanu, disguised himself to drink the amrita.

questions.q: Mohini is generally described in the texts as a being of what striking quality?
questions.explanation: Mohini is portrayed as a woman of irresistible, bewitching beauty.

questions.q: In assuming the Mohini form, Vishnu adopted which gender to accomplish his aim?
questions.explanation: Vishnu took on an entrancing female form as Mohini, his only female avatar.

questions.q: When Vishnu severed Rahu's head as Mohini served the nectar, what celestial bodies resulted from the two halves?
questions.explanation: Vishnu's discus split Rahu into the two shadow-planets Rahu (head) and Ketu (body).

questions.q: Which two luminaries exposed Rahu's deception by alerting Vishnu as Mohini poured the nectar?
questions.explanation: Surya and Chandra spotted the disguised Rahu and warned Vishnu, who then beheaded him.

questions.q: Because Surya and Chandra exposed him, Rahu is said in the Puranas to do what to them periodically?
questions.explanation: In revenge, Rahu and Ketu are said to swallow the Sun and Moon, causing eclipses.

questions.q: Which weapon of Vishnu is described as severing the head of the disguised demon Rahu?
questions.explanation: Vishnu used his Sudarshana Chakra to behead Rahu during the Mohini episode.

questions.q: Mohini is celebrated as which of Vishnu's avatars in terms of gender among his many incarnations?
questions.explanation: Mohini is traditionally regarded as the only female avatar of Vishnu.

questions.q: According to several Puranas, Mohini's enchanting form so captivated which great god that he too was drawn to her?
questions.explanation: Puranic accounts relate that even Shiva was enchanted upon beholding Mohini.

questions.q: In southern traditions, the union of Shiva and Vishnu-as-Mohini is said to produce which deity?
questions.explanation: Many southern texts hold that Ayyappa, also called Hariharaputra, was born of Shiva and Mohini.

questions.q: The deity Ayyappa, linked to Mohini, is also known by which epithet meaning 'son of Hari and Hara'?
questions.explanation: Ayyappa is called Hariharaputra, the son of Hari (Vishnu) and Hara (Shiva).

questions.q: During the churning of the ocean, which mountain was used as the churning rod before Mohini's appearance?
questions.explanation: Mount Mandara served as the churning rod during the Samudra Manthana.

questions.q: Which serpent-king served as the rope coiled around the mountain during the churning that led to Mohini's tale?
questions.explanation: The serpent Vasuki was used as the churning rope around Mount Mandara.

questions.q: Which tortoise avatar of Vishnu supported the sinking mountain during the same churning that preceded Mohini?
questions.explanation: Vishnu took the Kurma (tortoise) avatar to support Mount Mandara on his back.

questions.q: Mohini's central role in the nectar episode is essentially that of what?
options: A warrior who slays the demons
A judge who divides the nectar fairly
A deceiver who restores the nectar to the gods through enchantment
A sage who blesses both sides
questions.explanation: Mohini uses enchantment and guile to recover the nectar for the devas alone.

questions.q: Besides the Bhagavata Purana, the encounter between Shiva and Mohini is also notably recounted in which text?
questions.explanation: The Shiva Purana also narrates Shiva's encounter with Vishnu in the Mohini form.

questions.q: The classical Indian dance form said to embody the grace of Mohini's enchanting movements is called what?
questions.explanation: Mohiniyattam, the 'dance of the enchantress,' is named for and inspired by Mohini.

questions.q: In which great epic does an account of Vishnu assuming an enchanting female form to recover the nectar also appear?
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata's account of the churning describes Vishnu taking a bewitching female form.

questions.q: In one Puranic tale, Mohini is invoked to outwit the demon Bhasmasura, who had a boon to incinerate whomever he did what to?
questions.explanation: Bhasmasura could reduce to ash anyone whose head he touched; Mohini tricked him into touching his own.

questions.q: In the Bhasmasura tale, Mohini destroys the demon by luring him to do what?
options: Imitate her dance and touch his own head
questions.explanation: Mohini enchants Bhasmasura into copying her dance, leading him to touch his own head and burn to ash.

questions.q: The shadow-entities Rahu and Ketu, born from the Mohini episode, are counted among which group in Hindu astrology?
questions.explanation: Rahu and Ketu are reckoned among the Navagraha, the nine celestial influences.

questions.q: In the deeper philosophical reading found in the Puranas, Mohini personifies which concept that deludes the senses?
questions.explanation: Mohini is often interpreted as an embodiment of Maya, the bewitching power of illusion.

questions.q: Within Vishnu's traditional list of ten principal avatars (the Dashavatara), where does Mohini typically stand?
options: She is counted as the fifth avatar
She replaces Kalki as the tenth
She is not numbered among the ten principal Dashavatara
She is the first of the ten
questions.explanation: Mohini is a celebrated form of Vishnu but is not enumerated among the ten principal Dashavatara.
